page: Amazon Web Services m7i.2xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8488C processor operating at 3.2 GHz. The configuration provides 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It includes 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache resources consist of 48 KB L1d, 2 MB L2, and 105 MB L3 cache. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks position the instance primarily in the average tier for its hardware class. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests via stress-ng yield scores of 1869.09 and 7317.45 respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show 579.72 GB/sec for cached small blocks and approximately 50.51 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The instance demonstrates strong performance in text processing with a Geekbench score of 2359.0, though multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is relatively weak at 36.82 GB/sec. For lightweight machine learning tasks, it processes 1017.23 tokens/sec for 135M model prompts but drops to a weak 7.71 tokens/sec for 7B model text generation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m7i.2xlarge offers a balanced resource density of 4 GB RAM per vCPU, utilizing high-speed DDR5 memory and dedicated Intel Xeon cores without the added cost of local storage or GPU accelerators. This makes it a balanced option for workloads that do not require high-speed local NVMe drives. Recommended workloads include general-purpose applications, static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and Redis caching, where it achieves 10.81 million SET operations per second.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. Managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results place its compute and memory performance in the average tier, with a strong showing in text processing and a solid 14.35 GB/sec static web serving throughput. It delivers 10.81 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. This resource profile makes the m7i.2xlarge highly suitable for balanced workloads such as web serving, software compilation, database operations, and general-purpose application hosting where dedicated CPU execution and modern DDR5 memory are required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3 Gbps network baseline, optimized for balanced enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed DDR5 memory for reliable enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processor
32.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory with 4.0 GB per core
No local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage
3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor
Well-suited for static web serving, software compilation, and Redis database operations
